title: Microservices AI Review — Report Data
description: The evidence-based result data emitted by /ms-ai-review. This is the ONLY artifact the model produces; report/render.py injects it into report/template.html to generate report.html. The template is presentation-only and must not add, infer, or alter any finding.

commit.description: Short SHA of the assessed commit

stack.description: Detected stack, e.g. 'Java 17 / Spring Boot 3 / Kubernetes'

overall_score.description: Overall maturity score over auto-evaluated questions only

not_determinable.description: Questions not provable from code (reported as gaps, excluded from score)

na.description: Questions not applicable to this repo

responses.description: Response distribution for the doughnut chart

sections.description: Per-section scores — drives the radar and bar charts

short.description: Optional short label for chart axes (falls back to name)

status.description: ✓ Strong 4–5, ⚠ Needs work 2–4, ✗ Critical <2, n/a no scored questions

subdomains.description: Per-sub-domain scores — drives the detailed score table

score.description: 0–5, or null when all questions were NA / not-determinable

findings.description: Every No (and any low-confidence Yes/No). Each MUST carry real, cited evidence — no fabrication.

evidence.description: Concrete cited evidence (file:line or path) actually observed in the repo

gaps.description: Questions not determinable from code — reported, never guessed, never solicited

reason.description: Why it can't be proven from code (e.g. team competency, live-infra)
